definitions.text: Which, especially which of an open-ended set of possibilities.
examples: What colour are you going to use?
What time is it?
I wonder what colour he is going to use.
I know what colour I am going to use.
He seems to have lost what sense he had.
What money I earn is soon spent.

definitions.text: Which thing, event, circumstance, etc.: used in asking for the specification of an identity, quantity, quality, etc.
examples: Hammer in that nail —What with? I didn't bring my set of tools.
What is your name?

definitions.text: That which; those that; the thing(s) that.
examples: He knows what he wants and what to do to get it.
What is amazing is his boundless energy.
I will do what I can to help you.
What I'll do is, I'll phone you to let you know.

definitions.text: In what way; to what extent.
examples: What does it matter?
What do you care?

definitions.text: Used before a prepositional phrase to emphasise that something is taken into consideration as a cause or reason; usually used in combination with 'with' (see what with), and much less commonly with other prepositions.
An expression of surprise or disbelief.
examples: What! That’s amazing!
wait this song is so good whattt

definitions.text: What do you want? An abrupt, usually unfriendly enquiry as to what a person desires.
examples: What? I'm busy.

definitions.text: The identity of a thing, as an answer to a question of what.
Something that is addressed by what, as opposed to a person, addressed by who.
Emphasizes the truth of an assertion made to contradict an evidently false assumption held by the listener.
examples: Pitch contour: low [wʌt˩], low-dipping [wʌt˨˩]
— Sian sia, nearby here don’t have library.— The National Library is a five-minute walk from here what, no meh?

definitions.text: Used to invite agreement or acknowledgment when something is assumed to be straightforward or already obvious to begin with; similar in function to “right?” and “as you know”.
examples: Pitch contour: low [wʌt˩], low-dipping [wʌt˨˩]
If it doesn’t get sunlight, it won’t grow what, so you should consider moving it closer to the window.
